description:
Latent bug discovered while landing fz-3zx. Pre-3zx it was masked
because cont slot 0 was always typed `any` at cont seams; post-3zx
the LFP tightens slot 0 to `int` and the mismatch surfaces.

## The bug

A fn body whose terminator (on any reachable exit) is Term::Tail-
CallClosure uses a return_call_indirect against the closure-target
sig (§8.2: all-Tagged, including return). The forwarded return
value carries Tagged FzValue bits.

But that fn's OWN Cranelift outer sig declares return_reprs[sid]
based on its return Descr — int → RawInt, float → RawF64. The
indirect tail-call's Tagged bits flow out under a RawInt label.
Callers reading the result as RawInt see the tag-shifted bits
(e.g. 42 → 337 = (42<<3)|1).

Pre-3zx: every cont's slot 0 was typed `any` → schema FzValue →
the consumer side ALSO treated the return as Tagged → mismatch
self-corrected by being wrong-the-same-way on both sides.

Post-3zx: cont's slot 0 widens to `int` → schema RawI64 →
consumer reads raw → producer's Tagged label leaks as the
displayed value.

## Reproducer

  fixtures/apply2.fz prints 337 -55 instead of 42 -7.
  fixtures/higher_order.fz prints 337 -55 -79 instead of 42 -7 -10.
  fixtures/closure_typed_captures.fz: AOT SIGBUS (related).

All of these have a fn whose only exit is TailCallClosure.

## The fix

Codegen-side analysis: compute the transitive closure
`tagged_return_fns` of:

  Seed:
    - every closure_target_fn (already returns Tagged by fz-cps.1.8)
    - every fn whose any block terminator is Term::TailCallClosure

  Propagate:
    - if fn F has a block ending in Term::TailCall { callee } and
      `callee` is in tagged_return_fns, F joins tagged_return_fns

Then in the existing force passes:
  - return_reprs[sid]: force ArgRepr::Tagged when the spec's fn
    is in tagged_return_fns (extends the closure-target check
    at line ~2398).
  - tagged_slot0_cont_specs: include conts whose Term::Call's
    callee is in tagged_return_fns (extends the existing
    closure-target check at line ~2356).

## Acceptance

  - apply2.fz, higher_order.fz pass via JIT pre-3zx (no regression).
  - With fz-3zx applied on top, all 5 fixture goldens still pass.
  - sum_list_via_head_tail_recursion continues to pass.
  - No new lib-test regressions.

## Notes

Obsoletes fz-w5t (the previous catch-all "codegen seam
reconciliation" ticket — this is the specific fix).
title: fz-3zx.2: codegen — TailCallClosure-returning fns must declare Tagged return
